description: You are a Network Reliability Engineer at a Tier-2 ISP. Using the NET_ADMIN schema in Oracle (net_admin/NetAdmin2024, Host=localhost, Port=1521, Service=XEPDB1), perform forensic analysis to detect bogon leaks and BGP route hijacks and calculate IP footprints. Create a PL/SQL function `FUNC_IPV4_TO_INT(p_ip VARCHAR2)` to convert dotted-decimal IPv4 strings to 32-bit integers. Develop a view `BGP_PREFIX_RANGES_VW` (columns: route_id, prefix_base, prefix_length, origin_asn, start_ip_int, end_ip_int) calculating integer boundaries for routes in BGP_ROUTES. Identify bogon leaks in a table `BOGON_LEAKS` (columns: route_id, bogon_id, advertised_prefix as base/len, origin_asn, bogon_description) for routes falling entirely within BOGON_SPACE blocks. Detect route hijacks via a view `ROUTE_HIJACK_SUSPECTS_VW` (columns: victim_asn, victim_prefix, hijacker_asn, hijacker_prefix) identifying more specific routes covered by less specific routes with different origin ASNs. Calculate the unique IP footprint for each ASN in a materialized view `ASN_FOOTPRINT_MV` (columns: origin_asn, total_unique_ips), ensuring overlapping subnets from the same ASN are not double-counted. Automate the refresh of this materialized view daily at 2:00 AM using a procedure `PROC_REFRESH_FOOTPRINT` and a DBMS_SCHEDULER job `BGP_FOOTPRINT_JOB`. Export the results of `ROUTE_HIJACK_SUSPECTS_VW` to /home/ga/Documents/exports/bgp_hijack_report.csv.
